title: helianthemum vulgare subsp. glaucum
intro: Helianthemum vulgare subsp. glaucum, also known as Rockrose or Frostweed, is a perennial plant species in the Cistus family, characterized by its woody stems and pink to purple flowers. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Helianthemum vulgare subsp. glaucum include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Helianthemum vulgare subsp. glaucum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used topically in the form of creams, salves, or ointments.
